title: mentha spicata var. undulata
intro: Mentha spicata var. undulata, commonly known as corsican mint or wavy mint, is a subspecies of peppermint native to the Mediterranean region. It is a hardy perennial herb with a strong fragrance, typically used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The mentha spicata var. undulata plant has been used to treat digestive issues, coughs, and colds due to its antispasmodic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. It can be consumed as tea, used as a topical application, or added to herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which may help alleviate symptoms of nausea, headaches, and respiratory issues.
